techniques.evidence_quote: AWS Administrator is in search for a new job. Receives a job offer via Email from a recruiter. Downloads job offer on personal laptop. Opening job offer on laptop executed malware, stole credentials and cookies stored on Google drive
Attacker logs into AWS and executes ListBuckets commands across production AWS account
Opening job offer on laptop executed malware, stole credentials and cookies stored on Google drive
Opening job offer on laptop executed malware, stole credentials and cookies stored on Google drive
Creates test AWS access key and assigns Administrator role. Downloads csv with access key details and stores in local drive
stole credentials and cookies stored on Google drive
Attacker logs into AWS and executes ListBuckets commands across production AWS account
Attacker makes ~2000 requests to AWS API to: 1. Downloaded all S3 Buckets and corresponding file objects
2. Delete all S3 Buckets 3. Then, created a 'Ransomware' S3 Bucket

attack_chain.description: AWS Administrator (a former employee) received a spearphishing email disguised as a job offer from a recruiter and downloaded the malicious attachment on their personal laptop.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: AWS Administrator is in search for a new job. Receives a job offer via Email from a recruiter. Downloads job offer on personal laptop

attack_chain.description: The administrator opened the malicious job offer document on their personal laptop, which executed malware that stole credentials and browser cookies stored locally and synced to Google Drive.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Opening job offer on laptop executed malware, stole credentials and cookies stored on Google drive

attack_chain.description: The malware harvested browser session cookies and credentials from the personal laptop that were synced to Google Drive, including a previously created AWS Administrator access key CSV stored in the drive.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: stole credentials and cookies stored on Google drive. 4 months [after] Creates test AWS access key and assigns Administrator role. Downloads csv with access key details and stores in local drive

attack_chain.description: The attacker accessed the compromised Google Drive and retrieved the AWS Administrator access key CSV file that the administrator had previously downloaded and stored there — providing long-lived privileged programmatic AWS credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Creates test AWS access key and assigns Administrator role. Downloads csv with access key details and stores in local drive

attack_chain.description: Using the stolen AWS Administrator access key (long-lived credential), the attacker authenticated directly to the AWS production account without any additional authentication challenge.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Attacker logs into AWS and executes ListBuckets commands across production AWS account

attack_chain.description: The attacker executed ListBuckets API calls across the production AWS account to enumerate all S3 buckets and their contents, making approximately 2000 API requests total.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Attacker logs into AWS and executes ListBuckets commands across production AWS account. Attacker makes ~2000 requests to AWS API

attack_chain.description: The attacker downloaded all S3 buckets and their corresponding file objects via the AWS API, exfiltrating the organization's data.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Attacker makes ~2000 requests to AWS API to: 1. Downloaded all S3 Buckets and corresponding file objects

attack_chain.description: After exfiltration, the attacker deleted all S3 buckets across the production AWS account, destroying the organization's cloud data.
The attacker created a new S3 bucket named 'Ransomware' as a ransomware extortion signal, simulating a ransomware attack to pressure the victim into paying for data recovery.

initial_access_vector: Spearphishing email disguised as a job offer caused a former AWS Administrator to execute malware on their personal laptop, which stole credentials and cookies synced to a personal Google Drive — including a pre-existing AWS Administrator access key CSV stored there.
persistence_mechanism: None observed. The attacker leveraged a long-lived AWS Administrator access key (static credential) that had been created and stored in a personal Google Drive, providing persistent programmatic access without needing further foothold.
privilege_escalation: No escalation required. The stolen access key was already assigned the AWS Administrator role at the time of creation, granting the attacker immediate full administrative privileges upon use.

incident_root_cause: Phishing leads to compromise of credentials in former employee's personal Google Drive
